basics.name: Fabio Rocha Fernandes Távora
basics.label: Surgical Pathologist | PhD | CEO, Argos Laboratory
basics.summary: Surgical pathologist and scientist with subspecialty expertise in pulmonary, genitourinary, and cardiovascular pathology. Founder and CEO of Argos Laboratory, a reference anatomic and molecular pathology laboratory in Fortaleza, Brazil. Professor in the Graduate Program in Pathology at the Federal University of Ceará. Contributor to the WHO Classification of Tumours and member of the IASLC Pathology Committee.

work.name: Argos Laboratory — Anatomic and Molecular Pathology
work.summary: Reference regional surgical pathology laboratory with emphasis in oncologic pathology, processing over 100,000 specimens annually.
highlights: Co-founded and leads a multidisciplinary team of pathologists, molecular biologists, and histotechnicians
Oversees clinical operations, quality assurance, and digital pathology workflows
Leads integration of AI-assisted diagnostic tools and molecular testing platforms

work.name: Messejana Heart and Lung Hospital
work.position: Pathologist Consultant and Head of Surgical Pathology
work.summary: Reference center in Brazil for heart and lung transplants, studying approximately 4,000 specimens annually.
highlights: Renders diagnoses on surgical specimens with emphasis on thoracic and cardiovascular pathology
Member of the Scientific Advisory Board
Teaching of pulmonology and cardiology residents

work.name: Federal University of Ceará
work.summary: Graduate Program in Pathology — Mentor and supervisor of MSc and PhD students.
highlights: Supervises multiple graduate students in lung cancer, GU pathology, transplant, and digital pathology
PI on multiple active research projects

work.name: Armed Forces Institute of Pathology
work.position: Staff Pathologist, Department of Genitourinary Pathology
work.summary: Washington, DC. Chairperson: Isabell Sesterhenn, MD.

education.institution: Universidade Federal de São Paulo / EPM
University of Maryland School of Medicine

projects.name: Argos Laboratory
projects.summary: CEO and co-founder of a reference anatomic and molecular pathology laboratory in Fortaleza, processing over 100,000 specimens annually with emphasis on oncologic pathology.

projects.name: WHO Classification of Tumours
projects.summary: Contributor to multiple editions of the WHO Blue Books for Lung/Thymus/Heart (2014, 2021, 2025–26) and Genitourinary Tract (2021).

projects.name: IASLC Pathology Committee
projects.summary: Member of the International Association for the Study of Lung Cancer Pathology Committee (2025–2027), contributing to international consensus recommendations on lung cancer pathology.
